children.text: Compo Beach is where Westport’s coastal lifestyle feels most immediate. The neighborhood’s identity is tied to the shoreline and to the routines that come with it—morning walks, beach days, and an outdoors-first rhythm that’s hard to replicate inland.

Real estate here is primarily single-family, and buyers are typically drawn by proximity to the water and the overall lifestyle fit. In my experience, Compo Beach appeals to clients who want Westport’s broader amenities but want the home base to feel distinctly coastal.

Compo Beach (the park) is a major anchor. The Town of Westport lists it as a 29-acre park at 60 Compo Beach Road with an extensive sand beach, boardwalk, pavilion, concession stand, two sand volleyball courts, and a large wooden playscape, plus it’s adjacent to the Ned Dimes Marina. That combination makes the neighborhood’s lifestyle very tangible. Compo Beach is best for buyers who want a true shoreline routine within Westport.

highlights: Compo Beach Park - 29-acre town park with extensive sand beach and boardwalk
A true coastal Westport lifestyle with an outdoors-forward routine
Playground, concession stand, and volleyball courts at the beach
Strong fit for buyers prioritizing shoreline access and beach-day convenience
Beautiful waterfront location ideal for families and active lifestyles
name: Compo Beach
overview: Compo Beach is one of Westport’s signature coastal neighborhoods—defined by Long Island Sound access and a true beach-town rhythm (within a very polished town). Homes are primarily single-family, and the lifestyle is outdoor-forward, especially in summer. Compo Beach itself is a major amenity: a 29-acre town park with an extensive sand beach and a full roster of recreation facilities. For buyers who want Westport with the strongest “on the water” lifestyle, Compo Beach is the name to know.
